title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates a /goal command for OpenAI's Codex that enables autonomous code generation via a loop called Ralph. The tool accepts high-level English descriptions of desired outcomes and handles planning, execution, and self-correction without requiring user intervention at each step.
writeup: Jack Roberts demonstrates a /goal command for OpenAI's Codex that drives autonomous code generation through a loop called Ralph. The command accepts high-level English descriptions of a desired outcome, then handles planning, execution, and self-correction without user input at each step. The takeaway: a feedback loop turns a single goal statement into iterative code generation, shifting the developer's role from step-by-step prompting to specifying outcomes.
